userStories.title: Schema, DTO & i18n Extensions
userStories.description: Add graphifyEnabled flag and graphifyLastImportedAt timestamp to the Project model. Extend the source union in the RAG data model to include 'code'. Surface these fields in project DTOs with proper type mapping. Add i18n keys for the graphify-disabled error. This story lays the type foundation for all downstream stories — no service logic here.
acceptanceCriteria: Project.graphifyEnabled defaults to false when a project is created with no graphifyEnabled field
AddDocumentDto accepts source: 'code' without a validation error
AddDocumentDto rejects any source value not in ['ticket', 'doc', 'manual', 'code'] with a 400 validation error
IndexDocumentInput.source type includes 'code' as a valid literal
ProjectResponseDto.from(project) maps project.graphifyEnabled to dto.graphifyEnabled
ProjectResponseDto.from(project) maps project.graphifyLastImportedAt to dto.graphifyLastImportedAt as Date | null
UpdateProjectDto accepts graphifyEnabled: true without a validation error
i18n key rag.graphifyDisabled exists in both apps/api/src/i18n/en/rag.json and apps/api/src/i18n/zh/rag.json
suggestedCriteria: When ProjectResponseDto.from(project) is called and graphifyLastImportedAt is null, dto.graphifyLastImportedAt is null, not undefined
UpdateProjectDto rejects graphifyEnabled values that are not boolean with a 400 validation error
i18n key rag.graphifyImportSuccess exists in both en/rag.json and zh/rag.json with non-empty values
The Prisma migration is idempotent and can be applied multiple times without error

userStories.title: RagService Methods: deleteAllBySourceType & importGraphify
userStories.description: Add two new methods to RagService: deleteAllBySourceType(projectId, sourceType) to purge all documents of a given source type for a project, and importGraphify(projectId, nodes, links) to convert graph nodes to embeddable text, generate adjacency-aware content strings, and call indexDocument() for each node. Content generation produces deterministic text: '{type} {label} in {source_file}: {relation} {neighbor_label}, ...' for nodes with outgoing links; '{type} {label} in {source_file}' for isolated nodes with a source file; '{type} {label}' when source_file is absent. type defaults to 'node' when absent. Add unit tests for both methods.
acceptanceCriteria: RagService.deleteAllBySourceType(projectId, 'code') deletes all records where source = 'code' for the given project and returns the count of records deleted
RagService.deleteAllBySourceType(projectId, 'code') returns 0 when no source:'code' records exist for the project
RagService.importGraphify(projectId, nodes, links) calls deleteAllBySourceType(projectId, 'code') before indexing any nodes
RagService.importGraphify(projectId, nodes, []) with an empty links array indexes each node with content '{type} {label} in {source_file}' (source_file segment omitted when source_file is absent)
RagService.importGraphify(projectId, nodes, links) builds content that includes '{relation} {neighbor_label}' for each link where the node is the source
RagService.importGraphify(projectId, nodes, links) calls indexDocument() with source: 'code', sourceId: node.id, and metadata containing label, type, source_file, and community
RagService.importGraphify(projectId, [], []) returns { imported: 0, cleared: N } where N is the count returned by deleteAllBySourceType
suggestedCriteria: When a node has no source_file property, the content string omits the 'in ...' segment entirely
When a node has no type property, the content string uses 'node' as the type label
When importGraphify is called twice in succession with the same projectId, nodes, and links, the second call returns { imported: N, cleared: N } with no duplicate records
When importGraphify is called twice with the same nodes and links, the content strings produced are identical (deterministic given same input order)
When indexDocument() throws for a node, importGraphify continues to the next node and does not terminate early; the returned imported count equals nodes.length
deleteAllBySourceType called twice in succession returns the first call's count, then 0

userStories.title: Import Endpoint: POST /projects/:slug/kb/import/graphify
userStories.description: Add a new controller route POST /projects/:slug/kb/import/graphify to RagController. Guard it with ADMIN role. Create ImportGraphifyDto with GraphifyNodeDto and GraphifyLinkDto sub-DTOs. Call RagService.importGraphify(). On success, update project.graphifyLastImportedAt to the current UTC time via Prisma. Return { ret: 0, data: { imported, cleared } }. Reject imports when graphifyEnabled is false. Add e2e tests covering happy path, 403 non-admin, and 400 graphify disabled. After this story passes, regenerate openapi.json and the CLI generated client before starting US-005.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /projects/:slug/kb/import/graphify with a valid body and ADMIN auth returns { ret: 0, data: { imported: number, cleared: number } }
POST /projects/:slug/kb/import/graphify returns 403 when the caller does not have ADMIN role
POST /projects/:slug/kb/import/graphify returns 404 when the project slug does not exist
POST /projects/:slug/kb/import/graphify returns 400 when project.graphifyEnabled is false
POST /projects/:slug/kb/import/graphify with nodes: [] returns { imported: 0, cleared: 0 } and does not call indexDocument
ImportGraphifyDto rejects a request body missing the nodes field with a 400 validation error
ImportGraphifyDto accepts a request body with nodes present and links absent (links is optional)
The import route calls ragService.importGraphify(project.id, dto.nodes, dto.links ?? []) and returns its result
After a successful import, the controller updates project.graphifyLastImportedAt to the current UTC timestamp via Prisma
The next GET /projects/:slug response includes the updated graphifyLastImportedAt value
e2e spec covers: happy-path import with valid nodes and links returning 200, non-admin caller returning 403, graphifyEnabled false returning 400
suggestedCriteria: When POST /projects/:slug/kb/import/graphify receives nodes: [], deleteAllBySourceType is still called and the cleared count reflects prior code rows deleted
The graphifyLastImportedAt timestamp persists across subsequent requests and is not reset by unrelated project updates
Response Content-Type is application/json
ImportGraphifyDto ignores unknown top-level fields in the request body without error

userStories.title: Toggle Enforcement & Cleanup in ProjectsService
userStories.description: Inject RagService into ProjectsService. In the update() method, detect when graphifyEnabled transitions from true to false in the update payload. When this occurs, call ragService.deleteAllBySourceType(projectId, 'code') to purge stale code nodes. If deleteAllBySourceType throws, log at warn level and do not re-throw — the flag change persists regardless. Wire RagModule into ProjectsModule via imports. Add unit tests covering all toggle scenarios. This story can run in parallel with US-003 after US-002 is complete.
acceptanceCriteria: ProjectsService.update() calls ragService.deleteAllBySourceType(projectId, 'code') when graphifyEnabled changes from true to false in the update payload
ProjectsService.update() does not call ragService.deleteAllBySourceType when graphifyEnabled is not present in the update payload
ProjectsService.update() does not call ragService.deleteAllBySourceType when graphifyEnabled changes from false to true
ProjectsService.update() does not call ragService.deleteAllBySourceType when graphifyEnabled is true in both current state and update payload
When ragService.deleteAllBySourceType throws, ProjectsService.update() logs at warn level and does not re-throw the error (the flag change is persisted regardless)
RagModule is imported in ProjectsModule so RagService is available via dependency injection in ProjectsService
suggestedCriteria: When ProjectsService.update() is called and no cleanup is triggered, the project update succeeds normally
The graphifyEnabled flag change is persisted to the database before deleteAllBySourceType is called so that cleanup failure does not roll back the flag
Unit test mocks ragService.deleteAllBySourceType to throw and verifies the error is logged but not propagated to the caller
Unit test confirms deleteAllBySourceType is not called when the update payload omits graphifyEnabled entirely

userStories.title: CLI Command: koda kb import --graphify
userStories.description: Add an import sub-command to apps/cli/src/commands/kb.ts. Prerequisite: after US-003 merges, run `bun run api:export-spec && bun run generate` to regenerate openapi.json and the CLI generated client before implementing this story. Command signature: koda kb import --project <slug> --graphify <path-to-graph.json> [--json]. Steps: (1) Resolve auth via resolveAuth({}) — exit 2 if missing. (2) Read file at path — exit 1 with stderr message if not found or not valid JSON. (3) Parse { nodes, links } from the file; ignore all other top-level keys. (4) Configure API client and call the generated import service method. (5) On success: print human-readable message or JSON with --json flag; exit 0. (6) On API error: handleApiError(err). Add unit tests covering file validation, API calls, and output formatting.
acceptanceCriteria: koda kb import --project koda --graphify ./graph.json exits 0 and prints '✓ Graphify import complete: {imported} code nodes indexed ({cleared} cleared)' to stdout
koda kb import --project koda --graphify ./graph.json --json exits 0 and prints raw JSON response to stdout
koda kb import --project koda --graphify ./missing.json exits 1 and writes a file-not-found error message to stderr
koda kb import --project koda --graphify ./invalid.json (with non-JSON content) exits 1 and writes a parse-error message to stderr
koda kb import with missing --project flag exits 3
koda kb import with missing --graphify flag exits 3
When auth config is absent, koda kb import exits 2 without calling the API
The command sends { nodes, links } extracted from the parsed graph.json file and does not send other top-level keys from graph.json
suggestedCriteria: The command reads the entire file and validates JSON before making the API call, failing fast on file errors
Human-readable output on success includes both the imported count and the cleared count
The --json flag outputs the raw response object without additional wrapping
When graph.json has a links key absent or undefined, the command sends links: [] to the API

analysis:
This feature extends Koda's knowledge base (LanceDB) to ingest code-structure documents from Graphify's graph.json output. The architecture uses a push model: developers or CI pipelines build graph.json locally via `graphify .`, then push it to Koda via a new CLI command. The API ingests nodes/links, converts them to embeddable text chunks, and stores them with source:'code' in LanceDB alongside existing ticket and manual documents. No changes to the retrieval path are needed — existing hybrid search (RRF + FTS) handles the new source type automatically because source:'code' documents enter the same KB store.

**Source-of-truth discipline**: The current checkout may contain generated artifacts (dist/, apps/cli/src/generated/) that are ahead of the authoritative src/ tree and Prisma schema. Always treat src/ and prisma/schema.prisma as the source of truth. Do not edit generated files. After US-003 is complete, run `bun run api:export-spec && bun run generate` to regenerate openapi.json and the CLI generated client before starting US-005. This regeneration step is a hard prerequisite for US-005 — the CLI depends on the generated import service method.

**Key design decisions**:
1. Push model keeps the API server stateless and avoids running Python on the server.
2. source:'code' is added as a union variant in the RAG data model. Existing indexDocument() pipeline is reused with no new embedding provider or vector store.
3. Idempotent import: each import clears all prior source:'code' nodes for the project before inserting new ones. Re-running produces no duplicates.
4. Opt-in via Project.graphifyEnabled flag. When toggled from true→false, all code nodes are purged; cleanup failure logs at warn level but does not roll back the flag change (fail-open).
5. Content generation is deterministic given the same input order: '{type} {label} in {source_file}: {relation} {neighbor_label}, ...' for nodes with outgoing links; '{type} {label} in {source_file}' for isolated nodes; '{type} {label}' when source_file is absent. type defaults to 'node' when absent.
6. Fail-open per node: if embedding or indexing fails for a node, the existing fallback in indexDocument() stores a zero vector and continues. The import does not abort on partial failure. The returned imported count equals nodes.length (all attempted).

**Payload shape (from spec)**: GraphifyNodeDto requires id (string) and label (string); type, source_file, and community are optional. GraphifyLinkDto requires source and target (node IDs); relation is optional.

**DI cycle risk**: RagService must not depend on ProjectsService or ProjectsModule. The dependency direction is projects → rag only. Verify RagModule exports RagService before importing into ProjectsModule.

**Build order**: US-001 → US-002 → (US-003 ∥ US-004) → [regenerate openapi.json + CLI client] → US-005.
